steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls after "needn't" and before "they can still procure..." The sentence has two independent clauses joined by the transition word "though." The needed punctuation must correctly attach "though" while separating the two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "despair, though": A comma before "though" and nothing after leaves the second independent clause with no separator — a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses after a transitional word. This creates a run-on. ✗
  • "despair, though,": Placing commas on both sides of "though" treats it as a parenthetical inserted mid-sentence, but "though" here is not interrupting a single clause — it is connecting two independent clauses. Two commas do not properly separate two independent clauses. ✗
  • "despair, though;": A comma before "though" closes the first independent clause at the transition word; the semicolon after "though" then separates the two independent clauses. This is the correct SAT pattern — comma + transition + semicolon — when a transitional word sits between two independent clauses. ✓
  • "despair—though": An em dash before "though" with no punctuation after leaves the second clause unattached. The em dash does not fulfill the role of separating two independent clauses here. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
